vulnerability.title: Ensure Chrony is only configured with the server directive
vulnerability.description: Check that Chrony only has time sources configured with the server directive.
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Ensure Chrony is only configured with the server directive

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only requires editing the Chrony configuration file to ensure only 'server' directives are present and restarting the chronyd service. This is a straightforward change that does not affect authentication, SSH, critical services, or system boot. The risk of lockout or service disruption is minimal.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential time sync disruption if no valid NTP servers are configured

review_feedback: The sed command comments out any non-comment line that does not contain the word 'server', which will also comment out essential chrony directives such as driftfile, makestep, allow, and rtcsync. This can prevent chronyd from starting or operating correctly, leading to loss of time synchronization and possible downstream issues. No backup of the original configuration is made, increasing the risk of irreversible changes.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will comment out all non‑server directives, which will likely resolve the specific vulnerability but may also remove essential Chrony configuration such as driftfile, makestep, or rtcsync, potentially breaking the service.
concerns: The sed command is overly broad and will comment out any non‑comment line that does not contain the word 'server', including required configuration directives.
No backup of the original /etc/chrony.conf is created before modification.
Potential service disruption if essential directives are removed.
suggested_improvements: Restrict the sed pattern to only target time‑source directives (e.g., pool, peer, broadcast) rather than all non‑server lines.
Create a backup of the configuration file before editing, e.g., cp /etc/chrony.conf /etc/chrony.conf.bak.
Validate the Chrony configuration after changes using chronyd -Q or chronyd -t before restarting the service.
